title: Epistemic Compression Loop Record
description: One completed pass of the Epistemic Compression Loop over a single idea. See docs/philosophy/EPISTEMIC_COMPRESSION_LOOP.md for the protocol and .claude/skills/epistemic-compression/SKILL.md for the runnable procedure.

raw_perception.description: The original intuition, verbatim and unimproved. Preserved so later stages can be audited against what was actually perceived.

working_definition.description: A precise, non-circular definition of the idea. Must be falsifiable in principle: a stranger could classify examples as in/out.

nearest_analogies.description: Existing concepts, systems, or results this idea resembles, each with the point of resemblance and the point of divergence.

value_claim.description: What becomes possible, cheaper, or safer if the idea is true. States who benefits and by how much.

compressed_model.description: The idea restated at minimum length without losing load-bearing content. If compression loses nothing, the lost material was decoration.

testable_claims.description: Predictions with concrete observables. Each names what is measured, under what conditions, and what outcome the idea predicts.

falsifiers.description: Observations or experiment outcomes that would kill the idea. Each must be runnable or observable by someone other than the proposer.

replay_steps.description: Ordered steps by which a stranger with repo access reproduces the reasoning or the result: commands, file paths, inputs, expected outputs.

hidden_assumptions.description: Assumptions the idea silently depends on, surfaced by asking what must be true for each testable claim to even make sense.

variables.description: Named quantities, e.g. "kappa: spectral concentration of provider exposure".

relations.description: Claimed relationships between variables, as equations, inequalities, or monotonicity statements.

constraints.description: Domain bounds and invariants under which the relations are claimed to hold.

differs_by.description: The single structural change this variant makes to the compressed model.

discriminating_test.description: An observation that would distinguish this variant from the original.

verdict.description: Outcome of comparison against the original, if evaluated.

variants.description: Structural alternatives to the compressed model. Maturity requires at least three.

maturity_score.description: One point per gate passed: defined, compressed, testable, falsifiable, replayable, variant-compared.

decision.description: immature: gates 1-2 incomplete. testable: claims and falsifiers exist. provisionally_valid: all six gates passed. rejected: a falsifier fired or a variant dominates.

performative_cognition_flag.description: True when this pass increased confidence without producing sharper definitions, new testable claims, new falsifiers, executable replay steps, or better error detection.
